answer:
**Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*** (particularly chapters 4, 13, 20, and 23) explicitly addresses a communal Muslim "we" (the *ummah*, invoked as a transnational nation of believers, youth, and ancestors).[^1][^2][^3][^4] For instance:

- Chapter 13 ("Khitab ba Jawanan-e-Islam" / "An Address to the Muslim Youth") opens by directly hailing "O Muslim youth" and contrasts their failings with forebears, using "we have lost that inheritance we received from our ancestors."[^5]
- Chapter 23 ("Tarana-e-Milli") invokes a collective Muslim identity spanning "Chin to Arab lands," with "our story flows" and "we do not bow to what is false."[^6]
- Chapter 20 ("Shikwa" / "The Complaint") voices grievances from the Muslim community to God ("we are known for our acceptance of Your will"; "It was we... who were Your champions").[^7]
- Chapter 4 critiques contemporary Muslims ("You call yourselves Muslims?") in dialogue with the divine.[^8]

**Johann Gottlieb Fichte's *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*** (ch. 1) addresses European/German "peoples" ("you peoples") as a communal public arrayed against princes, urging collective resistance to retain freedoms ("No, you peoples, give up everything... only this you must uphold").[^9]

No other works in the corpus contain such explicit direct addresses to a national or communal "we" (e.g., no French treatises or Indic *smṛti* do; Old English poems use "we" poetically in narratives, not as communal hails). Searches for indicative phrases (e.g., "fellow citizens," "our nation," "O Muslims") surfaced no further matches.

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété*, [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/translation/].
[^2]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/.
[^3]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/.
[^4]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/23-tarana-e-milli/translation/.
[^5]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/#p-365462.
[^6]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/23-tarana-e-milli/translation/#p-8a4681.
[^7]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-01026e.
[^8]: /works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/04-dil-se-jo-baat-nikalti-hai-asar-rakhti-hai/translation/#p-bf18c2.
[^9]: /works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-20dba4.
